arts.ttl: Champions League live blog: PSG-Liverpool, Benfica-Barcelona
arts.sum: Enjoy live updates from Wednesday's Champions League action as Paris Saint-Germain take on Liverpool, Benfica take on Barcelona and Bayern take on Leverkusen.

arts.ttl: Liverpool BOMBSHELL: Reds made 'MONSTROUS' offer for PSG superstar
arts.sum: The Sky Italia journalist reports that Liverpool made a 'monstrous' €100m offer for Kvaratskhelia in the final days of summer 2024, just six months before he moved to PSG for €70m. The Reds face the Georgian and his new employers PSG in the Champions League on Wednesday evening and, in the build-up to the game, Italian transfer insider Gianluca di Marzio has dropped a veritable bombshell.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich, Liverpool & Man Utd learn asking price for Angelo Stiller
arts.sum: The major European clubs interested in Stuttgart midfielder Angelo Stiller face having to pay considerably more than a release clause in his current contract.

arts.ttl: Alexander Isak's Carabao Cup final hint as Newcastle lose another star for Liverpool clash
arts.sum: Alexander Isak came off during Newcastle's loss at the weekend, sparking fears he would miss the Carabao Cup final, but his involvement in media duties suggests he's expecting to play

arts.ttl: Liverpool, PSG’s Official Starting XI for Champions League Clash
arts.sum: Paris Saint-Germain are welcoming Liverpool to the Parc des Princes on Wednesday night. The Parisians are full of confidence and are hoping that can fuel them to secure an advantage before the scene shifts to Anfield next week. Manager Luis Enrique has plenty of players, especially those in the attack, that are in good form, […] The post Liverpool, PSG’s Official Starting XI for Champions League Clash appeared first on PSG Talk.

arts.ttl: Liverpool line-up vs PSG named as new injury blow confirmed
arts.sum: Arne Slot has named his XI for Liverpool's clash with Paris Saint-Germain in the Champions League at the Parc des Princes

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich Reacts to PSG’s Bold Offer for Arsenal, Inter Milan Target as Key Details Emerge
arts.sum: Paris Saint-Germain have been closely monitoring Bayern Munich midfielder Joshua Kimmich for months, nearly signing him last summer. Now, they are once again pushing and have reportedly made an offer. Journalist Christian Falk’s latest report on Kimmich’s future reveals that the veteran has received a solid offer from PSG. The Parisians are likely looking to […] The post Bayern Munich Reacts to PSG’s Bold Offer for Arsenal, Inter Milan Target as Key Details Emerge appeared first on PSG Talk.

arts.ttl: PSG’s Ousmane Dembele Named His 6 Football Idols - Included Heroic Liverpool Duo
arts.sum: Paris Saint-Germain winger Ousmane Dembele named two Liverpool greats in his list of six favourite footballers.

arts.ttl: PSG's post-Mbappe era, no 'clans' and foreign stars' French buy-in
arts.sum: Liverpool face a Paris St-Germain side in the Champions League who are hitting their best form of the post-Kylian Mbappe era.

arts.ttl: Paris Saint-Germain v Liverpool: Champions League last 16, first leg – live
arts.sum: Live updates from Parc des Princes, kick-off 8pm (GMT) Clockwatch: Bayern v Leverkusen and more | Email Scott Arne Slot talks to TNT Sports. “It’s a nice part of the schedule for the people at home watching football … PSG’s fans will be behind the team … it’s all about the quality PSG have … it’s going to be the toughest game in Europe we’ve played so far … in every aspect of the game they do things really well … their main threat is their three attackers … they don’t only have quality but are lightning fast … they can change direction so quickly … like the wingers at Manchester City … Mohamed Salah has added even more experience … he is calm and not frustrated if he misses a chance … he always knows how to hit the ball … instilling your will on PSG is almost impossible … they go all over you … they take risks and get a lot from that … we have to understand when to play … it’s not a game we will dominate in ball possession, I will be surprised if that happens tonight.” There’s no Cody Gakpo for Liverpool. He’s picked up an injury in training. The only change to the team that started last week’s 2-0 victory over Newcastle United is at left-back, where Andrew Robertson returns at the expense of Kostas Tsimikas, who drops to the bench. Continue reading...
